text:
**2. [Blocker — Phase E] "Replay from capsule alone" conflates scrubbing with re-execution.** `TransactionTrace` records what happened, payload-free; you cannot re-execute from traces. Re-execution requires the `DataTransactionScript` (operations *with payloads* — follow lists, filters), plus the same NMP version constructing the identical graph topology, because NodeIds are positional. Recording the script blows up the privacy surface far beyond the payload-free trace. Suggested change: define capsule v1 explicitly as **trace + receipts, scrub/reconstruction only**; document `replay_session(trace)` as reconstruction, not re-execution; list re-executable capsules (script capture) as an explicit non-goal or later phase with its own redaction story. [...] 2. **"Replay from capsule alone" was conflating two different things.** Traces are payload-free records — you can *scrub/reconstruct* from them, but you cannot *re-execute*. Re-execution would need payload-bearing scripts (follow lists, filters) plus identical graph topology per version — a much bigger privacy surface. The epic now pins capsule v1 to scrub-only and marks re-execution an explicit non-goal.
 [...] **3. [Blocker — Phase E, affects B] Traces are keyed by opaque NodeId/ScopeId — the capsule needs a symbolication manifest.** `changed_inputs`, `dirty_roots`, recompute lists, and most `AuditEvent`s are bare `NodeId`s. Without a NodeId/ScopeId → NMP-semantic-label registry captured at record time, a capsule replayed by an agent that never saw the session is unreadable, and cause chains can't be reconstructed. Suggested change: Phase E item — "capsule embeds a node/scope registry captured at record time; registry labels pass through the same local/shareable redaction modes." [...] **Other findings that changed the epic:** capsules need a **symbolication manifest** (traces are keyed by opaque NodeIds — unreadable to anyone who didn't see the session without an embedded label registry); Trellis trace versioning is exact-match-only, so the capsule needs its own **envelope version + compat policy** or every capsule attached to an old issue dies on the first format bump; the Phase D **transport decision moved into Phase A** because the lint-exemption wording depends on it; free-form reason strings became **structured reason codes** (type lints can't catch a Debug-formatted `ResourcePlan` leaking into a string); the receipt shape gained the fields its own examples required (owner-count *transition*, outcome status, parent scope, timestamps); my "agent answers why-is-my-feed-empty" exit criterion was rightly called untestable and is now a **seeded failure-scenario corpus** against a local relay fixture — deliberately including the "subscription fine, zero events came back" case, which receipts alone can't answer; and the **headless NMP host runner** is now itemized as its own (probably largest) Phase C work item. [...] **8. [Should-fix — Phase E] Redaction is mostly NMP-side work; the epic implies Trellis provides it.** `TraceRedactor` covers only step names, `ResourceKey`s, and invariant names. Pubkeys, relay URLs, and event ids live in NMP receipt fields and in ResourceKey *segments* (NMP taxonomy). Shareable mode also needs **consistent pseudonymization** — same key → same token capsule-wide, preserving the type segment (e.g. keep segments[0], hash the rest) — or refcount/causality analysis breaks after redaction. Suggested change: add "NMP-owned redactor over the receipt shape + ResourceKey segments, with capsule-wide stable pseudonyms" as an explicit deliverable. [...] **1. [Blocker — Phase E] The Trellis redaction hook leaks resource keys through the audit log.** `redact_trace` (crates/trellis-testing/src/scenario.rs:280-292) redacts `resource_commands`, `resource_coalescences`, and invariant names — but not `trace.audit_log`, and `AuditEvent::ResourceOpenCoalesced` carries a full `ResourceKey` (crates/trellis-core/src/transaction_types.rs:87-94). The epic cites these hooks as the redaction foundation; as implemented, a "shareable" capsule would ship un-redacted keys. Suggested change: add a Phase E checklist item — "upstream trellis fix: `redact_trace` must cover `audit_log` (and any future key-carrying fields); add a test that serializes a redacted capsule and asserts no original key material survives anywhere in the JSON." The "all prerequisites closed" claim is true of the listed issues, but this gap is new. [...] Assistant: Finding 1 confirmed: `redact_trace` covers `resource_commands`, `resource_coalescences`, and `invariant_results` — the `audit_log` is untouched. Checking the audit event and versioning claims: [...] 1. **Trellis's redaction hook leaks resource keys.** `redact_trace` covers resource commands, coalescences, and invariant names — but the audit log is cloned into every trace un-redacted, and `AuditEvent::ResourceOpenCoalesced` carries a full `ResourceKey`. A "shareable" capsule built on those hooks would ship the very keys you thought you'd scrubbed. Filed as [trellis#161](https://github.com/pablof7z/trellis/issues/161), now a hard gate for Phase E. [...] Everything is folded into [epic #2858](https://github.com/pablof7z/nostr-multi-platform/issues/2858) with a comment documenting the review pass, and the fabric room #xray-epic-review has the outcome logged in case @fable shows up for a second pass. The reviewer's verdict on the overall plan: phase ordering is right (ADR gate → receipts → CLI → pane → capsules); the weakness was concentrated in Phase E leaning harder on the trace foundation than the code supports — which is exactly what's now fixed in the text. [...] **Fable findings: already folded.** Epic #2858 was revised last round with all verified findings, trellis#161 was filed for the redaction leak — and I can see on the fabric that @codex4 already fixed it (draft PR [trellis#162](https://github.com/pablof7z/trellis/pull/162), awaiting review/merge). That upstream gate is nearly cleared. [...] **9. [Should-fix — Phase E] Versioning is exact-match, single-layer; the capsule has two layers.** `validate_format_version` (serialized.rs:193-202) hard-fails on anything but equality — "graceful" only in that it's a typed error, not migration. Every Trellis format bump orphans all outstanding capsules attached to issues, and the capsule also contains the NMP receipt/correlation shape, which versions independently. Suggested change: define a capsule **envelope** with its own version plus embedded `TRACE_FORMAT_VERSION`, NMP receipt schema version, and producer metadata (app version, NMP/Trellis versions, platform, recording window, redaction-mode marker); state a compat policy (reader supports N-1, or degrades to raw-JSON view Flight-Recorder-style instead of refusing). [...] **15. [Consider — Phase E] Residual privacy risk in shareable mode.** Even fully pseudonymized, interest-set sizes, per-relay subscription counts, and transaction timing fingerprint a user (follow count is basically recoverable). Note the residual risk in shareable-mode docs; consider bucketing counts in shareable exports. [...] **16. [Consider — Phase E] Ring buffer needs a byte bound, not just a count.** One teardown-cascade transaction can be enormous (the trace carries every command, frame, and audit entry). Bound by bytes and count. [...] **11. [Should-fix — Phase B] No overhead gating workstream.** `TransactionTrace::from_result` (trace.rs:54-89) deep-clones essentially the whole transaction result; a scrolling feed commits transactions at high frequency, so always-on recording in dev builds distorts the very behavior being debugged. Suggested change: Phase B item — recording is opt-in at runtime (disabled ⇒ zero clones, not "clone and drop"), with a measured per-transaction overhead budget and a benchmark gate. [...] - **Trellis trace-tooling cluster (very active right now):** #141 (versioned data scripts/trace files — closed #114), #83 ("Add transaction trace scenario replay"), #142 ("Build Flight Recorder trace replay demo"), **#160** ("Rebuild Flight Recorder as a trace workstation," merged today) — turned Flight Recorder into a full evidence workstation: provenance, transaction timeline, phase ladder, receipt inspector, replay/raw-trace tabs; explicit about `[BUNDLED FIXTURE]` vs `[LIVE UNAVAILABLE]` (no live WASM engine wired yet). This is functionally a time-travel/trace-replay UI already shipping under epic #87, distinct from and not yet connected to NMP's proposed nmp-devtools MCP surface (#2809) or chirp's X-Ray pane (#30). Closest existing artifact to "time-travel tooling." [...] One reuse note the surveyor surfaced: Trellis's **Flight Recorder workstation** (trellis#160, merged today) is already a full trace-replay UI — provenance, transaction timeline, receipt inspector, replay tabs. Phase E should feed NMP capsules into it for desktop-side inspection rather than building a second scrubber; the epic says so.
